subject: a long-haul trucker calling home at 3am
lyrics:
[Verse]
The diesel hums a lullaby outside the rest stop glow
Nebraska's just a black line where the mile markers go
I dial the number slow tonight, the receiver to my ear
Three a.m. and half asleep, I just need her to hear

[Verse]
She answers like she's underwater, voice all soft and frayed
The baby's finally down, she says, the porch light's still ablaze
I tell her 'bout the snow near Cheyenne, white as wedding lace
She tells me 'bout the drawing taped above the fireplace

[Chorus]
I'm a thousand miles of nothing and a tank that's running low
But your voice is the one road sign that tells me where to go
Keep the kettle on the stove, keep the kitchen light
I'll be home before the leaves turn, hold the world all right

[Verse]
The CB crackles static and the long night starts to lean
I count the dashes flashing by like every place I've been

[Bridge]
Don't hang up yet, just breathe with me a while
Let the silence hold us closer than a thousand mile
I can drive on empty coffee, I can drive on rain
Long as I can hear you breathing, I can stand the strain

[Chorus]
I'm a thousand miles of nothing and a tank that's running low
But your voice is the one road sign that tells me where to go
Keep the kettle on the stove, keep the kitchen light
I'll be home before the leaves turn, hold the world all right

subject: studying alone at two in the morning
lyrics:
[Verse]
Two o'clock and the lamp burns low (sha-la-la)
Pages turning soft and slow (oo-wee-oo)
Coffee gone cold in my hand
Trying hard to understand

[Verse]
The whole town's dreaming in their beds (bom-bom)
While these numbers fill my head (doo-wop, doo-wop)
Clock keeps ticking on the wall
Answer me, I'll give my all

[Chorus]
Oh, my love is a textbook page
And I'm burning up the midnight stage
Just me and a pencil and a prayer
Two in the morning, no one there
Sha-la-la, I'll see it through
Sha-la-la, I'm almost through

[Verse]
Morning's coming like a bell (ring-a-ling)
I know this lesson far too well (oo-wee-oo)
One more chapter, one more line
Then this lonely night is mine

[Bridge]
The streetlight's my only company tonight
Humming low beneath the amber light
I'll carry on, I'll carry on
Till the dark turns into dawn

[Chorus]
Oh, my love is a textbook page
And I'm burning up the midnight stage
Just me and a pencil and a prayer
Two in the morning, no one there
Sha-la-la, I made it through
Sha-la-la, I made it through

subject: the smell of a grandmother's kitchen
lyrics:
[Verse]
Morning break, the sun dey rise slow
Mama's pot dey sing on the low
Ginger, pepper, the steam dey climb
Take me back to a sweeter time
Wooden spoon dey turn the stew
Everything she touch turn new

[Chorus]
Grandma's kitchen, oh-oh-oh
Smell of home wherever I go
Jollof rising, spice in the air
Love you can taste, love you can wear
Grandma's kitchen, take me home
No matter how far I done roam

[Verse]
Smoke and laughter through the screen door
Bare feet dancing on the floor
Sweet plantain in the frying gold
Stories that the elders told

[Bridge]
Now the city loud and the rent dey high
But one small scent take me back, make I cry
Close my eyes, I'm seven again
Flour on her hands, rain on the tin

[Chorus]
Grandma's kitchen, oh-oh-oh
Smell of home wherever I go
Jollof rising, spice in the air
Love you can taste, love you can wear
Grandma's kitchen, take me home
No matter how far I done roam

subject: coming home from the war
lyrics:
[Verse]
Wheels touch down on the runway grey
Eighteen months I been away
Duffel bag and a thousand-yard stare
Civilian air, I forgot how to breathe it in

[Verse]
The terminal lights are too loud, too bright
Everybody moving, nobody's in the fight
My hands still flinch at a slamming door
Don't know what I'm running from anymore

[Chorus]
Run, run, the engine in my chest
Faster than my heart can rest
I'm coming home but the home's not here
It's somewhere in the noise and the fear
Break it down, let the bassline carry me through
Back to a version of me I once knew

[Verse]
Mama at the gate with a homemade sign
Arms wide open at the border line
I hold her tight but my mind's still away
In a desert town on a dust-red day

[Bridge]
Turn it down, turn it down, let the silence land
Somebody hold the shaking out my hand
The war is over but it lives in me
Gonna take some time to set it free

[Chorus]
Run, run, the engine in my chest
Faster than my heart can rest
I'm coming home and I'll learn it slow
How to put the weapon down and let it go

subject: a barber who knows the whole town
lyrics:
[Verse]
The bell above the door rings sweet and low
Velvet chair and the radio soft and slow
Clippers hum a tune that the whole town knows
That's where every secret in the city goes

[Chorus]
Old Sammy with the steady hand
Knows the heartbeat of the land
A fade, a shave, a word of grace
The wisest man in the wisest place
He knows your name, he knows your blues
He knows the road before you choose

[Verse]
The mayor and the milkman take the selfsame seat
Leave their troubles in a pile at his careful feet
He nods, he listens, never says too much
But the whole town's softer for his gentle touch

[Verse]
Mirror catches forty years of comin' and gone
Babies turned to fathers while the chair stayed on

[Bridge]
So pour the talc and tip your hat
There's more than just a haircut where the old man sat
He stitched a town together thread by thread
With a comb, a blade, and the kind words he said

[Chorus]
Old Sammy with the steady hand
Knows the heartbeat of the land
A fade, a shave, a word of grace
The wisest man in the wisest place

subject: a tailor who dresses the whole block
lyrics:
[Verse]
Needle through the linen in the back of the shop
Dusty fan turning, vinyl crackle won't stop
Chalk lines on the shoulder, pins between his teeth
Making kings out of corner boys underneath
Mr. Okafor been here thirty-one years
Measured every prom suit, dried a few tears

[Chorus]
He dress the whole block, seam by seam
Stitching folks into who they dream
Tape around the heart, not just the waist
Every thread a little grace
Slow drums, dusty light
He got the city looking right

[Verse]
Funeral black and the wedding white
He cut 'em both with the same care, same light
Kid came in slouching, walked out tall
A good fit can hold up a man through it all

[Bridge]
The mannequin watches the rain on the glass
While the radio whispers and the hours pass
He presses the lapel, lets the steam rise slow
Dressing up a neighborhood the world won't know

[Chorus]
He dress the whole block, seam by seam
Stitching folks into who they dream
Tape around the heart, not just the waist
Every thread a little grace

subject: a lighthouse keeper's love letters to the sea
lyrics:
[Verse]
Soft the morning on the salt-white stair
I fold a letter in the ocean air
No address but the rolling blue
Every word of it I write for you

[Chorus]
Oh my sea, my restless love
Grey below and gold above
I keep the lamp and you keep me
Writing letters to the sea
La-da-da, the tide comes in
Kissing salt against my skin

[Verse]
Forty years I climbed this winding tower
Lit the flame at the lonely hour
Never married, never far
Wed the water and the morning star

[Verse]
The gulls deliver what the waves won't say
I seal my heart and send it on its way

[Bridge]
They say the lighthouse soon will run on its own
A blinking eye and the keeper gone
But who will write the sea goodnight
Who will tell her she's the light

[Chorus]
Oh my sea, my restless love
Grey below and gold above
I keep the lamp and you keep me
Writing letters to the sea

subject: texting someone you shouldn't
lyrics:
[Verse]
Three little dots upon the screen
Late-night message, you know what I mean
My thumb hover over the send
Know this road don't have no good end

[Chorus]
But I text you anyway, oh-oh
Know it's wrong but I can't let go
Trouble waiting on the other line
One more message, one more time
I shouldn't, I shouldn't, but I do
Can't stop my heart from reaching you

[Verse]
The wise man inside me say leave it alone
Put down the phone, leave it well alone
But the foolish heart it beat so loud
Typing in the dark beneath the cloud

[Verse]
Delivered, then the bubble appear
Now my whole night gonna disappear

[Bridge]
My mama warned me 'bout this kinda fire
Don't go feeding what you should retire
But the moon so full and the night so long
And it feel so right when I know it's wrong

[Chorus]
But I text you anyway, oh-oh
Know it's wrong but I can't let go
Trouble waiting on the other line
One more message, one more time

subject: the last night of a festival
lyrics:
[Verse]
The sun gone down on the final day
Dust on the boots, the bands all play
Strangers turn to family by the speaker tower
Holding on tight to the dying hour

[Chorus]
Last night, last night of the festival
Raise your hands, give your all
We won't sleep till the morning come
One heart beating like a drum
Last night, hold it close
This the moment matter most

[Verse]
Fairy lights strung through the smoke and the trees
Mud on my knees and the warm night breeze
A girl I just met singing every word
Most alive I ever heard

[Verse]
The headline act bring the whole field down
Forty thousand voices a single sound

[Bridge]
Tomorrow we scatter to the four wide winds
Back to the cities and the jobs and the sins
But tonight we are one, tonight we are free
Tonight is forever, just you and me

[Chorus]
Last night, last night of the festival
Raise your hands, give your all
We won't sleep till the morning come
One heart beating like a drum

subject: a getaway driver's last job
lyrics:
[Verse]
Engine running, headlights low
Meter on the dash, two minutes to go
This the last one, I swear it's the last
Then I'm driving toward a clean new past

[Pre-Chorus]
My hands are steady, my heart is not
Give me the signal, give it all you got

[Chorus]
Drive, drive, into the neon rain
One more night, then I lose this name
Foot to the floor and the city blurs by
Last job, baby, then we fly
Drive, drive, don't look behind
Leave the whole broken world behind

[Verse]
Three quick knocks and the back door slam
Go, go, go — and away we ran
Sirens bloom in the rear-view glass
Pedal down, let the old life pass

[Bridge]
There's a beach somewhere with your name in the sand
A quiet life I been trying to plan
Just get us clear of these city lights
And I'll never drive another night

[Chorus]
Drive, drive, into the neon rain
One more night, then I lose this name
Foot to the floor and the city blurs by
Last job, baby, then we fly

subject: a chess hustler in the park
lyrics:
[Verse]
Five dollars on the table, son, sit on down
I'm the fastest set of hands in this whole town
Pigeons scatter, the clock starts to tick
You think you're clever, but I'm quicker than quick

[Chorus]
King me, take me, lay your money down
I been ruling this old bench like a crown
The park's my kingdom, the board's my throne
Every pawn out here belong to me alone
Checkmate, college boy, that's the game
You'll learn my street and you'll learn my name

[Verse]
Grey beard, sharp eyes, a coffee gone cold
Been playing these squares since the bench got old
Wall Street suits come to test their nerve
They get the lesson they deserve

[Verse]
Slap the clock, talk a little trash
Trade you a knight for a fistful of cash

[Bridge]
They don't know I sleep where the subway hums
They don't see the winter when the money don't come
But on these sixty-four squares I'm a millionaire
King of the pigeons and the cold park air

[Chorus]
King me, take me, lay your money down
I been ruling this old bench like a crown
The park's my kingdom, the board's my throne
Every pawn out here belong to me alone

subject: a tailor who dresses the whole block
lyrics:
[Verse]
Slide into the shop where the soul music plays
He been cutting cloth since his younger days
Smooth hands moving like he's writing a song
Making you feel like you finally belong

[Chorus]
He dress the whole block, oh yeah
Make a grown man stand up and care
From the corner kid to the deacon's pew
He tailor a dream right onto you
Silk and satin, baby, fit just right
He got the whole neighborhood shining tonight

[Verse]
She came in heartbroke, head hanging low
He stitched her a dress and she found her glow
Walked out the door like she owned the street
Confidence cut to a perfect fit, so sweet

[Pre-Chorus]
It ain't just fabric, it ain't just thread
It's the way you hold the crown on your head

[Bridge]
Ooh, every seam a little prayer
Every measure say somebody care
He don't just dress you, he set you free
Show you the king and the queen you can be

[Chorus]
He dress the whole block, oh yeah
Make a grown man stand up and care
From the corner kid to the deacon's pew
He tailor a dream right onto you

subject: coming home from the war
lyrics:
[Verse]
Stepped off the bus with my boots still tight
City looking different in the morning light
Got the groove in my walk but a weight in my chest
Home at last but I can't seem to rest

[Chorus]
I'm back, I'm back, put the bassline down
Feel my feet remember this town
Shake off the dust, shake off the fear
Thank the good Lord I'm standing here
Get up, get down, I survived the night
Now I'm dancing back into the light

[Verse]
My baby met me at the corner store
Didn't say a word, just hit the floor
Wrapped me up in a funky embrace
Welcome home written all over her face

[Verse]
The block threw a party with the horns out loud
Lifted me up on the shoulders of the crowd

[Bridge]
But at three in the morning when the music stop
I still hear the engines and the rotor chop
So I play this groove till the demons run
Dance away the things I seen and done

[Chorus]
I'm back, I'm back, put the bassline down
Feel my feet remember this town
Shake off the dust, shake off the fear
Thank the good Lord I'm standing here

subject: falling for someone from your hometown
lyrics:
[Verse]
I flew from the city on a Friday night
Little old hometown, same old light
Walked into the diner where I used to sit
And there you were, oh, that was it

[Chorus]
Fell for you, hometown girl, fell hard and fast
Funny how the future looks just like the past
Grab my hand, let's swing across the floor
I ain't ever leaving this old town no more
Boom-ba-da-boom, my heart's keeping time
Fell for a sweetheart who was here all this time

[Verse]
We used to ride bikes down on Maple Street
Never knew you'd sweep me off my feet
Now you're laughing with a milkshake in your hand
And I'm the happiest fool in the land

[Verse]
The jukebox spinning something old and true
Every single note keep pointing to you

[Bridge]
All those years I chased the bright big-city dream
When the realest thing was right here all the time, it seems
So pour me a coffee, save me a dance
Give a hometown heart a second chance

[Chorus]
Fell for you, hometown girl, fell hard and fast
Funny how the future looks just like the past
Grab my hand, let's swing across the floor
I ain't ever leaving this old town no more

subject: a grandmother's hands
lyrics:
[Verse]
Quiet morning, light through the lace
A cup of tea and a weathered face
I watch her hands as the kettle sings
The stories held in those small worn things

[Verse]
Knuckles like the roots of an ancient tree
They held my father, they held me
They kneaded bread through the leanest years
They wiped away a hundred tears

[Chorus]
Oh, her hands, her hands
Maps of every place she's ever been
Oh, her hands, her hands
Gentle rivers underneath the skin
Hold on, hold on, while the light still stands
Hold on to your grandmother's hands

[Verse]
Now they tremble as she pours the tea
Slower than they ever used to be
But they reach for mine across the years
And everything I am is here

[Bridge]
When the swell comes in and the strings all rise
I see whole decades behind her eyes
The garden, the war, the children grown
A lifetime carried in skin and bone

[Chorus]
Oh, her hands, her hands
Maps of every place she's ever been
Oh, her hands, her hands
Gentle rivers underneath the skin
Hold on, hold on, while the light still stands
Hold on to your grandmother's hands
